column1: # compare.tsv — the `cmp` three-way comparison, exhaustive over scalars.
# `a cmp b` yields -1 / 0 / 1 — a sorts before / with / after b, the
# same total order as lt / gt / sort (see eng/go/compare.go).  The rows
# are the full cross product of a representative value of each scalar
# kind and every scalar type literal.
# Two anomalies are visible in the data and recorded here as-is:
#  - Number-branch TYPE LITERALS (Number / Integer / Decimal) read as 0
#    through the numeric comparator, so they compare equal to each other
#    (the Boolean literal collapses with `false` the same way).
#  - the bare `Scalar` root literal has no scalar branch rank, so it
#    falls to the size/type fallback on a different scale from the
#    branch-rank order — making CompareValues NON-transitive when it is
#    involved: `Scalar cmp -2` is 1 and `-2 cmp true` is 1, yet
#    `Scalar cmp true` is -1.  A known bug, not intended behaviour.
